title: ficus populnea f. planicostata
intro: Ficus populnea f. planicostata, also known as the Fig tree or Banyan Fig, is a species of fig tree native to tropical regions of the world, characterized by its broad, flat leaves and unique spreading habit. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ficus populnea f. planicostata include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of diabetes, as well as its use as an anti-aging agent. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
